title: Evaluation of GFP-SopF expression on ATGlyation using spinning disk microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"udq1","text":"The protocol describes the microscopy-based analysis of ATGlyation of cell culture cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mount 24-glass bottom well on a Yokogawa CSU-W1 spinning disk confocal on a Nikon Eclipse Ti2-E motorized microscope equipped with a Nikon Plan Apochromat 40×/0.40 N.A air-objective lens, Nikon Plan Apochromat 60×/1.42 N.A oil-objective lens and a Plan Apochromat 100×/1.45 N.A oil-objective lens. Signals of 405/488/568/647 fluorophores were excited in sequential manner with a Nikon LUN-F XL solid state laser combiner ([laser line – laser power]: 405 nm - 80 mW, 488 nm - 80 mW, 561nm - 65 mW, 640 nm - 60 mW using a Semrock Di01-T405/488/568/647 dichroic mirror. Fluorescence emissions were collected with Chroma ET455/50m [405 nm], 488 Chroma ET525/50m [488 nm], 568 Chroma ET605/52m [561 nm], 633 Chroma ET705/72m [640 nm] filters, respectively (Chroma Technologies). Confocal images were acquired with a Hamamatsu ORCA-Fusion BT CMOS camera (6.5 µm2 photodiode, 16-bit) camera and NIS-Elements image acquisition software.
Seed HeLaTMEM192-HA cells into 24-well 1.5 high performance glass bottom plates (Cellvis, 24-1.5H-N, 20k per well).
Wash three times with PBST. Exchange PBST for 1xPBS for imaging.
Segment lysosome (Lamp1), ATG8s (LC3B or panGABRAP) and SopF (GFP) channels. Relate these signals to each other. Measure object parameters of choice (number, overlap etc).
The next day, transfect cells with GFP-SopF (RRID:Addgene_137734) using FuGene HD transfection reagent (Promega) according to manufacturer protocol. Exchange full media the day after transfection.
Check GFP-expression using a fluorescent microscope
Two days after the transfection, treat cells were for 24 h with 2 µM U18666A to block NPC1 function.
Fix cells using either ice-cold methanol (10 minutes, for LC3B) or paraformaldehyde (30 minutes, for panGABARAP).
For panGABARAP staining, permeabilize cells with 0.5 % Triton X-100 in PBS for 15 minutes at room temperature.
Wash samples three times with 0.02% Tween20 in PBS (PBST), and block for 10 minutes in 3% BSA-1xPBS at room temperature and wash again three times in PBST.

Incubate for 3h in primary antibodies against GFP (chicken), Lamp1 (mouse) and LC3B / panGABARAP (both rabbit) in 3% BSA-1xPBS. Primary and secondary antibodies used in this study can be found in the Key Resource Table.
Wash three times with PBST. Incubate for 1 h in secondary antibodies against chicken (Alexa 488), mouse (Alexa 568) and rabbit (Alexa 647)
Using the Nikon Plan Apochromat 60×/1.42 N.A oil-objective lens, acquire 6 µm stacks of all channels.

Perform image quantification was in your tool of choice. Here we will use ImageJ/FiJi and custom-written batch-macros and CellProfiler pipelines for quantitative evaluation. Use Fiji to create single-color gray-scale MIPs from each channel for CellProfiler. Import images into CellProfiler and assign them to cellular structures to be evaluated.
Export measurements as .csv files. Perform statistical analysis as well as plotting in Prism.
creator: Felix Kraus
